summary: iter-99 verified end-to-end. Backend pytest 26/27 PASS (1 FAIL → Devanagari name with combining marks rejected by current regex). Profile MUST FRs verified: original failing Rushikesh payload now 200, name accepts digits/hyphen/apostrophe/dots, name rejects empty/1-char/>80 chars, address <10 chars → 400, address ≥10 → 200, phone validation unchanged (rejects 1-5 leading + non-10-digit + non-numeric, accepts 6-9 leading). Negative extend_days MUST FRs: extend_days:-5 pulls end_date back 5 days, extend_days:-9999 clamps to sub.start_date FLOOR (exact match within 2s), audit doc persists SIGNED -7 (not abs). Positive extend_days regression + iter-98 meals_delta ± regression all green. Frontend Playwright (1920x1080, /admin/wallet-topup): wallet-days-dec/-extend-days/-inc trio present, default 0, apply correctly disabled w/o values, disabled w/o reason, enabled with (extendDays=-3 + reason), stepper math correct (3× dec = -3, then 5× inc = +2), signed input accepts typed -10, POST body sends `extend_days: -10` signed. Cleanup: user_7db238e7de98 end_date restored to original via +10 day compensate. user_session and TEST_IT99* admin docs purged.

critical.issue: NAME_RE `^[\w\s\.\'\-]{2,80}$` (re.UNICODE) does NOT match Devanagari names that contain combining marks (matras). Python's \w matches Lo (Other_Letter) but NOT Mn (Mark_Nonspacing) or Mc (Mark_Spacing_Combining). Confirmed in REPL: 'राहुल' → False, 'अमित' → False, 'नमस्ते' → False; only mark-less names like 'अजय' pass. Review request explicitly listed Devanagari support as a MUST FR. Fix: switch to the `regex` third-party module (supports \p{M}) OR expand char class to include Unicode mark categories explicitly, e.g. `^[\w\s\.\'\-\u0300-\u036F\u0900-\u097F\u0980-\u09FF\u0A80-\u0AFF\u0B80-\u0BFF\u0C00-\u0C7F\u0C80-\u0CFF\u0D00-\u0D7F]{2,80}$` or simply add `\u0900-\u097F` (and other Indic blocks) which include both letters AND their matras.

action_items: Expand _NAME_RE in /app/backend/routes/auth.py to include Indic combining marks. Recommended: `^[\w\s\.\'\-\u0900-\u097F\u0980-\u09FF\u0A80-\u0AFF\u0B80-\u0BFF\u0C00-\u0C7F\u0C80-\u0CFF\u0D00-\u0D7F\u0300-\u036F]{2,80}$` (covers Devanagari + Bengali + Gujarati + Tamil + Telugu + Kannada + Malayalam + general combining diacritics). Re-run iter-99 pytest to confirm 27/27 green.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/auth.py:148 — `re.UNICODE` flag is redundant on Python 3 (default). The real gap is that \w doesn't pick up combining marks. A comment claiming 'Unicode-aware (Devanagari …)' is technically misleading since matras still fail. Either fix the regex or update the comment to clarify 'plain letter + digit Unicode'.

routes/auth.py:158 — Error message says 'letters / digits / spaces / . - ' allowed' but doesn't mention which scripts are allowed, leading to silent rejection of non-Latin users. Consider returning a 'name_invalid' error code plus localised hint.

server.py:2352 — `new_end = old_end + timedelta(days=int(payload.extend_days))` uses int cast. WalletAdjustRequest.extend_days is Optional[int] so Pydantic already coerces; the int() is belt-and-suspenders, fine.

server.py:2353 — Floor compares datetimes via `new_end < start`. Works because both come from parse_dt → naive datetimes, but if start_date and end_date ever have different tz info this becomes a TypeError. Consider normalising both to UTC-naive at parse time.
AdminWalletTopup.jsx:91 — guard `!amt && !extendDays && !mealsDelta` correctly treats 0 as falsy and any non-zero (including negative) as truthy. Good.

AdminWalletTopup.jsx:284 — min/max -365..365 on extendDays input matches inc/dec clamp. Good parity with backend (no server-side cap exists for extend_days though, so a power admin could POST extend_days:5000 directly — consider a server-side `abs(extend_days) <= 3650` sanity check).

success_rate.backend: 96% (26/27 — 1 Devanagari-matra fail, all other MUST FRs green)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (9/9 UI FR assertions for the wallet-days stepper trio)

context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-99 backend pytest covers: profile (original payload, name accept/reject, address min 10, phone unchanged) + negative extend_days (–5 / –9999 floor / signed audit / positive regression) + iter-98 meals_delta regression. Re-run after fixing _NAME_RE to confirm 27/27. UI test cookie injection at preview domain still works (sameSite=None, secure, httpOnly). admin/users listing is keyed by `wallet-user-{user_id}` testid; the user labelled 'Rohan 23 / 8421372391' IS user_7db238e7de98 (confirmed via Mongo lookup).
rca of the issue: Backend `_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"^[\w\s\.\'\-]{2,80}$", re.UNICODE)` only matches what Python's \w considers alphanumeric. In Unicode terms \w = L*+Nd+Pc, but does NOT include Mark categories (Mn = non-spacing marks, Mc = spacing combining). Devanagari vowel signs ('matras') like ा ि ु े ो and the virama ् are all classified as Mn or Mc, so any Devanagari name with a non-inherent vowel (i.e. almost every real name) fails validation. Repro: `python3 -c "import re; print(bool(re.compile(r'^[\w\s\.\\\'\-]{2,80}$', re.UNICODE).match('राहुल')))"` → False. Mitigation: expand the char class with Indic block ranges (\u0900-\u097F covers Devanagari letters + matras + virama). Or switch to the third-party `regex` module which supports `\p{L}\p{M}`.
